titleStmt.title: SII 23.59: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 59. (A.R. No. 59 of 1907.) Kāṇippākkam, Chittoor Taluk, Chittoor District Manikaṇṭhēśvara temple—on the same wall

summary.p: This inscription records the remission of some taxes due on the dēvadāna lands, and also the gift of the incomes like āyam, āḷamañji etc. on the same by the chief Śiṟaimīṭṭa-Perumāḷ Śīyagaṅgar in favour of the temple of Maṇivaṇḍīśvaram-Uḍaiyār at Kāvaṇippākkam. Śiṟaimīṭṭa-Perumāḷ which means “the Lord who freed from imprisonment” as applied to this chief should have some significance. It is possible that this Śīyagaṅga was a son of Amarābharaṇa mentioned in No. 57 above and a feudatory of Rājarāja III on whose behalf he might have fought against Kō-Peruñjiṅga when he was imprisoned by the latter at Śēndamaṅgalam.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastīśrī . kāvaṇippākkattu Āḷuṭaiya nāyaṉār
maṇivaṇṭiśvaramuṭaiyār tevatāṉan ciṟai
miṭṭaperumāḷāṉa ciyakaṅkar Eppeṟpaṭṭa varikaḷum
Āyaṅkaḷum Āḷamañci maṟṟu māṭṭum maṇivaṇṭiśvara
muṭaiyāṟke viṭṭeṉneṉṟu tirumukaṅ kuṭuttaru
ḷukaiyiṉāl nām mu tai ceṭṭiyeṉ nānum ti
rumukappaṭiye viṭṭeṉ Ittai Iyiṟakkuvāṉ
keṅkaikaraiyiṟ kurāṟpacuvaik koṉṟāṉ pā
vattiṟp paṭuvāṉ .
Facing this inscription is an animal looking like a cow.
Digital edition of SII 23.59 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
